title: Don’t Be Triggered — Qur’an, emotions, and self-mastery
stanceSummary: Al-Baqarah 2:216 separates what we like from what truly benefits us; traditional psychology’s “emotional logic” versus detachment and compassion maps onto that retraining. The speaker urges treating the Qur’an as the operating manual for resetting reactive defaults and not handing one’s inner world to provocation or manipulation. Captions are cleaned, not proofread—verify with the recording.
alternateQuestions: What does the Qur’an say about liking what harms you?
How can I stop being emotionally triggered?
How does Islam teach self-control over anger?
What does it mean that Allah made people a trial for each other?
